regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean briefing for March 28, 2026: San Juan is currently 74°F with light ESE winds at 4 knots under overcast skies and a 55% chance of rain. Expect thunderstorms later today with ENE winds increasing to 9 knots and a 73% chance of rain, continuing through the next three days with persistent tropical moisture and scattered showers across ports including St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor radar for embedded thunderstorms; small craft operators should exercise caution in exposed anchorages due to gusty winds. Cruise passengers should plan for intermittent wet weather and possible brief delays in outdoor activities.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 28, 2026: Cozumel reports 78°F with NNE winds at 10 knots under partly cloudy skies and no rain currently. Expect increasing northeast winds to 14 knots today with a rise in rain chance to 63 percent, then scattered showers continuing through Day 3 as winds shift easterly at 13 knots. Captains should prepare for reduced visibility and occasional wet decks, especially near coastal areas like Costa Maya and Belize City where showers may affect anchorage comfort. Cruise guests advised to carry rain gear ashore and monitor local updates; fishing operations should plan for intermittent showers and moderate seas but no marine warnings are in effect.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 28, 2026: Currently, skies are clear across Nassau with light NNE winds at 4 knots and temperatures near 67°F. Today will warm to around 80°F with NNE winds increasing to 10 knots, and only a slight chance of rain (3%), making it ideal for day cruises and fishing near Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini. By tomorrow, expect overcast skies with ENE winds rising to 22 knots; a 31% chance of rain suggests securing loose gear and monitoring local updates, especially in exposed anchorages like Berry Islands. On day three, conditions will deteriorate with E winds at 24 knots and drizzle likely at 45% rain chance, warranting caution for small craft operators and advising passengers to plan indoor activities in Turks & Caicos and surrounding ports. No marine warnings are in effect but increasing winds may prompt a Small Craft Advisory by day three.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather for March 28, 2026, remains stable with light east to east-northeast trades at 14-16 knots and temperatures near 80°F. Aruba, Curaçao, Bonaire, and Cartagena will experience partly cloudy to overcast skies with minimal rain chances under 10% today; expect a slight increase to 25% chance of showers by day three. Mariners should anticipate moderate easterly winds supporting smooth seas; no Small Craft Advisories or warnings are in effect. Fishing captains should plan early departures to avoid the increasing cloud cover and light showers forecast later in the week, while cruise and yacht operations can maintain normal schedules with routine weather monitoring. Anchorage conditions remain favorable across all ports with no significant weather impacts expected.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions this morning show a cool 78°F with northeast winds at 11 knots and overcast skies at Grand Cayman. Rain chances remain low today near 13 percent, but a moist tropical disturbance will increase rain showers and northeast to east-northeast winds to 16-19 knots through March 30. Expect periods of moderate rain and choppy seas, especially at exposed anchorages like Belize City and Roatan; small craft operators should exercise caution and monitor conditions closely. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for wet decks and reduced visibility on days two and three, while passengers are advised to carry rain gear and limit outdoor activities during showers.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles outlook for March 28, 2026: Barbados reports 78°F with east winds at 12 knots under partly cloudy skies and a low 15 percent chance of rain. Expect modestly increasing cloud cover regionwide with east to east-northeast winds steady near 12-14 knots over the next three days; rain chances remain low, peaking at 20 percent today. Seas are moderate, with no Small Craft Advisories in effect. Mariners should maintain standard caution while navigating open waters near St. Lucia, Martinique, and Dominica, especially in exposed anchorages where easterly winds may increase vessel motion. Cruise and fishing operations can proceed as usual, but remain alert for brief showers and localized gusts in overcast conditions.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region conditions this morning feature light WNW winds at 2 knots, 56 degrees, and partly cloudy skies with no rain threat. Expect fog near the coast late today despite a high near 76 degrees and increasing WNW winds to 9 knots; visibility may be reduced around Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach harbors, so exercise caution during early evening navigation. Tomorrow brings warmer temperatures up to 83 with a 10-knot westerly breeze and overcast skies; slight chance of rain remains negligible. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor fog development near Catalina Island and maintain vigilant lookout; passenger vessels should advise travelers to dress in layers and anticipate limited visibility periods in port approaches. No marine warnings are in effect.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada area conditions this morning are cool at 59°F with calm west winds and widespread fog reducing visibility. Expect a slow improvement with afternoon temperatures reaching mid-70s, southwest winds 5-8 knots, but fog may linger near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos through the day. Over the next three days, mild, mostly overcast conditions prevail with light winds under 10 knots and minimal rain chance under 5 percent. Mariners should exercise caution entering or leaving Ensenada harbor and anchorages due to limited visibility; fishing and yacht captains should plan for restricted sightlines early and monitor updates for fog clearance before offshore operations.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region will experience mild northwest winds 10-12 knots through March 28-30 with temperatures steady near 75°F and overcast skies. Seas remain moderate with no advisories in effect, providing favorable conditions for navigation and fishing operations around Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Yacht and fishing captains should maintain standard watch protocols as northwest winds could cause slight chop near exposed anchorages. Passengers can expect comfortable, stable weather with no rain; light layers are recommended for the overcast conditions.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region conditions for March 28, 2026, are stable with light southwest winds near 1 knot and partly cloudy skies. Expect overcast conditions with west to west-northwest winds increasing to 9-11 knots over the next three days, with minimal rain chances below 10 percent. Mariners should find calm seas in Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata harbors, with no advisories in effect. Fishing and yacht captains can plan normal operations, but monitor wind shifts as overcast skies may reduce daytime visibility.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is currently experiencing overcast skies with light northeast winds at 2 knots and minimal rain risk today. Winds will increase from the west-northwest to 12 knots tomorrow, bringing scattered rain showers with an 18 percent chance of precipitation. Rain chances spike to 80 percent on March 29 with continued WNW winds near 9 knots, so captains should prepare for reduced visibility and patchy showers, particularly around Punta Mita and Yelapa anchorages. Conditions improve on March 30 with lighter winds and partly cloudy skies; however, fishing operations should monitor updated forecasts for possible wet weather and adjust schedules accordingly.
regions.western-mediterranean:
Western Mediterranean daily briefing for March 28, 2026:

Current conditions in Barcelona show light WNW winds at 5 knots, 51°F, and partly cloudy skies with no rain risk. Expect a gradual warm-up today with SW winds increasing to 7 knots and overcast skies; light rain is possible near Barcelona and Valencia with a 25% chance. Tomorrow, N winds strengthen to 16 knots with thunderstorms likely, raising rain probability to 38%; small craft operators should exercise caution, especially near Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza where conditions may deteriorate quickly. By day three, winds shift WNW at 8 knots with stable overcast and no rain expected—good conditions for fishing or yacht anchorage in Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Passengers should prepare for variable weather and possible wet decks on day two; captains should monitor updates for thunderstorm development and maintain secure mooring.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather for March 28, 2026: Naples currently reports 53°F with light NNE winds at 3 knots, partly cloudy skies, and no rain expected today. Light northerly winds will persist through the next two days with overcast conditions across the region, including ports from Marseille to Venice. Expect rain showers to develop by day three, particularly impacting Naples and nearby coastal areas, with a 66% chance of precipitation and winds shifting northwest at 8 knots. Mariners should maintain normal operations but monitor forecasts closely for increasing rain and reduced visibility near Naples and Civitavecchia after 48 hours; small craft should exercise caution when venturing offshore during the showers. Passengers can plan shore excursions today with minimal weather disruption but carry rain gear later in the week.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 28, 2026:

Current conditions in Athens/Piraeus show mild temperatures near 56°F with light WSW winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies. Visibility is good with no rain expected today, favorable for port operations and yacht movements around Athens, Santorini, and Mykonos. Fog is expected tonight and into tomorrow morning near Piraeus and possibly in low-lying coastal areas such as Rhodes and Izmir, so mariners should exercise caution when navigating near shore or entering harbors. Rain showers are forecast for March 29 with light NW winds under 10 knots, posing minimal impact but requiring routine vigilance for fishing captains operating near Cyprus and Haifa. Moderate southerly winds return by March 30, accompanied by fog again, warranting careful watchkeeping during early departures or arrivals.
